figures.caption: Figure 5: CoinRun median normalized performance (Eq. equation 1). Shaded area depicts 0.25 and 0.75 quartiles of 5 seeds. Bold line segments indicate training of task. (A) Default order of tasks (one-cycle). (B) Reversed order of tasks (one-cycle). (C) Default order of tasks (two-cycle). The dotted vertical line marks the end of cycle 1 and the beginning of cycle 2.
Table A.8: CoinRun single-task sample-efficiency (raw rewards). Best performance is written in bold.
Table C.2: General training and experimental parameters.

Figure 4: Atari metrics shown as median with (0.25 - 0.75) quartile confidence intervals, across 5 seeds, and calculated using normalized scores (Eq. 1). (A) Default task order (one-cycle). (B) Reversed task order (one-cycle). (C) Default task order (two-cycle).

Figure 1: World Model Learning. (A) Images drawn from the replay buffer are encoded to and reconstructed from a latent space using a recurrent state space model. (B) Learning the policy is achieved with Actor (A) and Critic (C) networks applied to latent states “dreamt-up” by the model.
Table A.2: Training schedule and evaluation protocol.

Figure 6: CoinRun metrics shown as median with (0.25 - 0.75) quartile confidence intervals, across 5 seeds, and calculated using normalized scores, Eq. 1. (A) Default order of tasks (one-cycle). (B) Reversed order of tasks (one-cycle). (C) Default order of tasks (two-cycle).
Table C.4: Experimental run breakdown across benchmarks and methods.
Table A.5: Atari (Normalized) continual learning sample-efficiency. Best performance is written in bold.
Table A.7: Atari single-task sample-efficiency (raw rewards). Best performance is written in bold.
Table A.6: CoinRun (Normalized) continual learning sample-efficiency. Best performance is written in bold.

Figure 3: Atari median normalized performance (Eq. 1). Shaded area depicts 0.25 and 0.75 quartiles of 5 seeds. Bold line segments indicate training of task. (A) Default order of tasks (one-cycle). (B) Reversed order of tasks (one-cycle). (C) Default order of tasks (two-cycle). The dotted, vertical line marks the end of cycle 1 and the beginning of cycle 2.

Figure A.1: Performance of DreamerV3, with bold line segments denoting the periods in which certain tasks are being trained. Scores are normalized using min-max normalization. The line is the median and the shaded area is between the 0.25 and 0.75 quantiles, of 5 seeds.

Figure 2: Experiment setup. (A) Augmented buffer used in ARROW. (B) Continual learning tasks with and without shared structure. NB: no background, RT: restricted themes, GA: generated assets, MA: monochrome assets, CA: centered agent.
